change_comments.message: very good! Just 2 tiny issues left
-1 just for you to answer my question.
all good just confusion about the header values...

plz check the testware.

[ERROR]   MdcServletFilterSpec.Should set Mapped Diagnostic Context from header if present:40->Should set Mapped Diagnostic Context from header if present_closure1:43 Condition not satisfied:

MDC.get('REQUEST_ID_HEADER') == 'test-request-id'

class org.slf4j.MDC

inline_comments.message: NB: can be extracted to a constant and same constant can be used to fetch the headers and also put the same to the MDC map.
please check u still need to populate the context with UUID if the headers not present?? any benefit in doing so ??
use MDC.remove and just remove what you are populating.
this can be moved outside the test.

expand the meaning of MDC just once.
Mapped Diagnostic Context

use single quotes.
"X-ONAP-RequestID" --> 'X-ONAP-RequestID'

similarly follow at other places. in groovy we use single quotes.
the body of the commit message can be a little descriptive here.
this is not properly indended. check other tests how they are formatted.
no need for prefix in context of this class

not sure we want to refer to ONAP here, it could be any stack of onap and non-onap components. Also you are mixing snake case with title-lcase?
I suggest to use CPS instead e.g. X-CPS-Request-Id
no need for prefix in context of this class

stay consistent with above e.g
X-CPS-Client-Id
according to the documentation null-check is not needed MDC.put() handles null (by removing it)
not using gString functionality so use single ' (everywhere in this test)
never import .* check your IDE settings please
still some " that should be changed to ' :-)

I hope you have checked with DT on this. Coz i assumed the header name was defined somewhere else and we need to adhere to it.
I asked earlier and I recall that Rajesh mentioned that it is pre-decided. please help to double check this.

NB: Id
same with ClientId as well.
I checked and this naming follows the standard convention for custom headers. We also had a demo with DT and they were fine with this naming for headers.
remove extra spaces here and line #42
I checked and this naming follows the standard convention for custom headers. We also had a demo with DT and they were fine with this naming for headers.
ignore this comment, it is a reply to another comment at https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/140715/comment/87b80825_61a06dcc/
you reverted these names but I thought your previous message on this (see above) indicated DT was ok with the proposed new names included the term CPS ?!

ok so just to eliminate any confusion.
X-CPS-Request-Id and X-CPS-Client-Id is approved to be used. right?

also in groovy we use single quotes.
update everywhere in this test.
if the request-id and client-id are set in MDC why are we asserting with null ??
During filter chain -assert MDC has value. After filter chain complete assert MDC is null/cleared.

This is not a slogan. Do not mention expectations or conditions in  basic test slogan
in this case 'MDC Filter change handling' will do
during the filter chain call MDC only contain the expected headers

you are mocking behavior here, not asserting!
it shodu start with: 1 *
assert that MDC does NOT contain the 'Other Header'
instead mock any possile header, see sampel shared with Priyank

it took me a while to see what is happening here its a bit unusual but is seems to work.
I dont think I did anything like this ever before but you are assert the content of the static MDC map at teh moment this call is made...
(did you verify when you run it with a change value that it fail?!
where was this agreed? or how did this lead to this values? wiki and jira doesnt say much
Thanks, Lee. These updated values are from the wiki page, so it's properly captured for future reference.

The values were agreed upon with DT in internal discussions. I see the same has not been updated in ONAP wiki. @Gourav Malviya please do the needful and update the documentation.

Note for future:
Please don't mark comments resolved before the issue is fixed as it may cause confusion for peer reviewers 😊
